hacks.title: 2-Minute Rule
hacks.description: If a task takes less than 2 minutes, do it immediately. This prevents small tasks from piling up.

hacks.title: Pomodoro Technique
hacks.description: Work for 25 minutes, then take a 5-minute break. After 4 cycles, take a longer 15-30 minute break.

hacks.title: Eat the Frog
hacks.description: Do your hardest task first thing in the morning when your willpower is strongest.

hacks.title: Time Blocking
hacks.description: Schedule specific blocks of time for different tasks. Treat these blocks like appointments.

hacks.title: The 5-Second Rule
hacks.description: When you have an impulse to act on a goal, count 5-4-3-2-1 and physically move before your brain stops you.

hacks.title: Active Recall
hacks.description: Instead of re-reading notes, close the book and try to recall information from memory. This strengthens retention.

hacks.title: Spaced Repetition
hacks.description: Review material at increasing intervals: 1 day, 3 days, 1 week, 2 weeks. Use apps like Anki.

hacks.title: Feynman Technique
hacks.description: Explain a concept in simple terms as if teaching a child. If you can't, you don't understand it well enough.

hacks.title: Study Before Sleep
hacks.description: Review important material right before bed. Your brain consolidates memories during sleep.

hacks.title: The Cornell Method
hacks.description: Divide your notes into 3 sections: notes, cues, and summary. Review cues to test yourself.

hacks.title: 7-Minute Workout
hacks.description: A quick high-intensity circuit: jumping jacks, wall sit, push-ups, crunches, step-ups, squats, tricep dips, plank, high knees, lunges, push-up rotations, side plank.

hacks.title: Walk After Meals
hacks.description: A 10-15 minute walk after eating helps digestion and regulates blood sugar levels.

hacks.title: Desk Stretches
hacks.description: Every hour, do neck rolls, shoulder shrugs, wrist circles, and stand up to stretch your back.

hacks.title: Stair Climbing
hacks.description: Always take stairs instead of elevators. It's free cardio that adds up throughout the day.

hacks.title: Morning Stretch Routine
hacks.description: Start your day with 5 minutes of stretching: cat-cow, child's pose, forward fold, and gentle twists.

hacks.title: Box Breathing
hacks.description: Breathe in for 4 seconds, hold for 4, breathe out for 4, hold for 4. Repeat 4 times to reduce stress.

hacks.title: Gratitude Journal
hacks.description: Write 3 things you're grateful for each morning or night. This rewires your brain for positivity.

hacks.title: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding
hacks.description: When anxious, name 5 things you see, 4 you hear, 3 you feel, 2 you smell, 1 you taste.

hacks.title: Digital Sunset
hacks.description: Stop using screens 1 hour before bed. Read a book or journal instead for better sleep.

hacks.title: Morning Pages
hacks.description: Write 3 pages of stream-of-consciousness thoughts every morning to clear your mind.

hacks.title: STAR Method
hacks.description: Answer interview questions with: Situation, Task, Action, Result. This structures your responses clearly.

hacks.title: LinkedIn Optimization
hacks.description: Use keywords from job descriptions in your profile. Recruiters search by keywords.

hacks.title: Coffee Chat Networking
hacks.description: Reach out to 1 person per week for a 15-minute virtual coffee. Build relationships before you need them.

hacks.title: Skill Stacking
hacks.description: Combine 2-3 average skills to become unique. Writing + Marketing + Data = Content Strategist.

hacks.title: Weekly Review
hacks.description: Every Friday, review what you accomplished, what you learned, and plan next week's priorities.

hacks.title: 50/30/20 Budget
hacks.description: Allocate 50% of income to needs, 30% to wants, 20% to savings. Simple and effective.

hacks.title: 24-Hour Rule
hacks.description: Wait 24 hours before any non-essential purchase over $50. Most impulse urges fade.

hacks.title: Automate Savings
hacks.description: Set up automatic transfers to savings on payday. You can't spend what you don't see.

hacks.title: No-Spend Days
hacks.description: Challenge yourself to spend $0 on certain days. Pack lunch, skip coffee shops, stay in.

hacks.title: Subscription Audit
hacks.description: Review all subscriptions monthly. Cancel anything you haven't used in 30 days.

hacks.title: Active Listening
hacks.description: When someone talks, focus fully. Don't plan your response. Summarize what they said before replying.

hacks.title: 5:1 Ratio
hacks.description: For every negative interaction, have 5 positive ones. This ratio predicts relationship success.

hacks.title: Weekly Date Night
hacks.description: Schedule dedicated time with your partner weekly. No phones, no distractions.

hacks.title: I-Statements
hacks.description: Say 'I feel...' instead of 'You always...'. This reduces defensiveness in conflicts.

hacks.title: Appreciation Text
hacks.description: Send one genuine appreciation message to someone daily. Strengthens all relationships.

hacks.title: 8-8-8 Rule
hacks.description: Aim for 8 hours of work, 8 hours of personal time, 8 hours of sleep. Balance is key.

hacks.title: Water First
hacks.description: Drink a full glass of water immediately upon waking. Rehydrates your body after sleep.

hacks.title: 20-20-20 Rule
hacks.description: Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Reduces eye strain.

hacks.title: Sleep Schedule
hacks.description: Go to bed and wake up at the same time daily, even weekends. Regulates your circadian rhythm.

hacks.title: Meal Prep Sunday
hacks.description: Prepare meals for the week on Sunday. Saves time, money, and ensures healthier eating.

hacks.title: Inbox Zero
hacks.description: Process emails to zero daily. Archive, delete, respond, or create a task. Don't let emails pile up.

hacks.title: Single-Tasking
hacks.description: Focus on one task at a time. Multitasking reduces productivity by up to 40%.

hacks.title: Mind Mapping
hacks.description: Start with a central idea and branch out related concepts. Great for brainstorming and studying.

hacks.title: Teach to Learn
hacks.description: Explain what you learned to someone else. Teaching is the best way to solidify knowledge.

hacks.title: Body Scan Meditation
hacks.description: Lie down and mentally scan from head to toe, noticing sensations. Releases tension and stress.

hacks.title: Worry Time
hacks.description: Schedule 15 minutes daily to worry. Outside this time, postpone worries. Reduces anxiety.

hacks.title: Cold Shower Finish
hacks.description: End your shower with 30 seconds of cold water. Boosts alertness and immune system.

hacks.title: Standing Desk Intervals
hacks.description: Alternate between sitting and standing every 30-60 minutes. Reduces back pain and increases energy.

hacks.title: Salary Negotiation
hacks.description: Always negotiate. Research market rates, state your value, and ask for 10-20% more than offered.

hacks.title: Round-Up Savings
hacks.description: Use apps that round up purchases to the nearest dollar and save the difference automatically.
